generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor for real-time measurement of indoor temperature and humidity levels in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, integrated into Building Control Systems and modeled within BIM environment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureHumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT occupancy sensor for real-time tracking of human presence in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, providing data for adaptive control systems and energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance AirQualitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structural sensor for monitoring indoor air quality parameters (CO2, VOCs, particulate matter) in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, supporting occupant comfort and environmental compliance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualityDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyMonitoringDevice : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Device for real-time energy consumption monitoring in HVAC and lighting systems across LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, enabling data-driven optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralEnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprehensive sensor network for structural and operational monitoring (vibration, pressure, equipment status) in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, integrated with BIM semantic attributes."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralDataTransmitted
]

instance LightingSystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor network for real-time lighting system monitoring in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, tracking energy usage and operational status for adaptive control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureHumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance AirQualityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualitySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyMonitoringDevice
]

instance StructuralDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralEnvironmentalSensor
]

instance LightingDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingSystemSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BuildingInformationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Foundational BIM model enriched with semantic metadata for Building Control Systems components (sensors, actuators, controllers) serving as the unified semantic layer for LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library Digital Twins."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToDTTransformationEnabler
]

instance BehavioralControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic model representing control logic and device configurations for HVAC and lighting systems in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, enabling adaptive operational responses."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicDeploymentEnabler
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computational model for simulating indoor environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, air quality) based on real-time sensor data and occupancy patterns in academic and library facilities."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance EnergyOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Predictive model for energy consumption optimization based on occupancy patterns, environmental data, and historical trends in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyOptimizationEnabler
]

instance TemperatureHumidityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured dataset containing real-time temperature and humidity measurements from IoT sensors across LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, normalized and enriched with BIM spatial metadata."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureHumidityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalAnalyticsEnabler, ControlLogicDeploymentEnabler
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy data collected from sensor networks in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, used for adaptive control and energy optimization decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyOptimizationEnabler, ControlLogicDeploymentEnabler
]

instance AirQualityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Comprehensive dataset of indoor air quality parameters (CO2, VOCs, particulate matter) from structural sensors in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, supporting occupant comfort analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AirQualityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance EnergyConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of energy consumption from HVAC and lighting systems in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, used for real-time monitoring and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyOptimizationEnabler
]

instance StructuralData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structural and operational data from environmental sensors (vibration, pressure, equipment status) in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, integrated with BIM for comprehensive monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralHealthMonitoringEnabler
]

instance LightingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Operational data from lighting systems in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, including energy usage patterns and status information for adaptive control."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LightingDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LightingControlEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMToDTTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering tool that automates the transformation of enriched BIM models into deployable Digital Twin artifacts, supporting semantic interoperability between physical and digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables LEEDPlatinumBuildingDTService, UniversityLibraryDTService
]

instance ControlLogicDeploymentEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Deployment framework for behavioral control models that translates dynamic control logic into executable configurations for HVAC and lighting systems with minimal manual intervention."
    DTDFVocab:enables HVACAdaptiveControlService, LightingAdaptiveControlService
]

instance EnvironmentalAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time analytics engine processing indoor environmental data to generate predictive insights about occupant comfort and air quality conditions in academic and library facilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables IndoorEnvironmentMonitoringService, AirQualityForecastingService
]

instance EnergyOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization engine leveraging machine learning to analyze energy consumption patterns and recommend control adjustments for maximum efficiency in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library."
    DTDFVocab:enables EnergyConsumptionOptimizationService
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structural monitoring system analyzing vibration, pressure, and equipment status data to detect anomalies and predict maintenance needs in building infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralIntegrityMonitoringService
]

instance LightingControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated control system for lighting infrastructure that adjusts based on occupancy and ambient light conditions to optimize energy usage in academic and library environments."
    DTDFVocab:enables LightingAdaptiveControlService
]

instance EclipseBasyxIntegration : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source Industry 4.0 platform integration enabling modular digital twin development, real-time data exchange, and interoperability between building systems and external services."
    DTDFVocab:enables LEEDPlatinumBuildingDTService, UniversityLibraryDTService
]

instance ItwinJsVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization library supporting collaborative digital twin development with real-time data synchronization and spatial data integration for facility management applications."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveDashboardService, ARVRVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance LEEDPlatinumBuildingDTService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Comprehensive Digital Twin service for LEED Platinum Academic Building providing real-time monitoring, optimization, and control of indoor environmental systems with research-oriented capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides LEEDPlatinumBuildingMonitoringInsight, EnergyOptimizationRecommendationAction, HVACAdaptiveControlAction
]

instance UniversityLibraryDTService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Digital Twin service for University Library focusing on real-time indoor condition monitoring, energy optimization, and adaptive control of building systems with research validation capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides LibraryEnvironmentMonitoringInsight, EnergyOptimizationRecommendationAction, LightingAdaptiveControlAction
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring service tracking indoor environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, air quality) across both academic and library facilities with interactive visualization capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides IndoorEnvironmentStatusInsight
]

instance EnergyConsumptionOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing energy consumption optimization based on occupancy patterns and environmental data, with predictive analytics for demand forecasting."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyConsumptionInsight, EnergyOptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance HVACAdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated control service for HVAC systems that adjusts operational parameters in real-time based on occupancy and environmental conditions to maintain optimal comfort and efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACPerformanceInsight, HVACAdaptiveControlAction
]

instance LightingAdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated lighting control service that adjusts based on occupancy and ambient light conditions to optimize energy usage while maintaining adequate illumination levels."
    DTDFVocab:provides LightingEnergyInsight, LightingAdaptiveControlAction
]

instance AirQualityForecastingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive service forecasting indoor air quality conditions based on real-time sensor data and historical patterns to support proactive environmental management."
    DTDFVocab:provides AirQualityForecastInsight
]

instance StructuralIntegrityMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring service analyzing vibration, pressure, and equipment status data to detect anomalies and predict maintenance requirements in building infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralHealthInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance InteractiveDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Customizable visualization service providing real-time dashboards for researchers, facility managers, and building occupants to monitor system states and receive alerts."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateVisualizationInsight
]

instance ARVRVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization service enabling AR/VR overlays on BIM models for spatial decision-making, equipment inspection, and collaborative troubleshooting in building management."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveVisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance LEEDPlatinumBuildingMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational insights about indoor environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, air quality, occupancy) in LEED Platinum Academic Building, supporting data-driven research and facility management."
]

instance LibraryEnvironmentMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring insights about indoor conditions (temperature, humidity, lighting, occupancy) in University Library, enabling optimized facility operations and research validation."
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive status overview of indoor environmental parameters across all monitored spaces, including comfort metrics and air quality indicators with historical trend analysis."
]

instance EnergyConsumptionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detailed energy consumption analytics showing real-time usage patterns, peak demand periods, and efficiency metrics for HVAC and lighting systems in academic and library facilities."
]

instance HVACPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance metrics for HVAC systems including efficiency ratings, operational status, and comfort level indicators based on real-time sensor data and control logic execution."
]

instance LightingEnergyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Energy usage analytics for lighting systems showing consumption patterns, occupancy-based adjustments, and efficiency metrics across different zones in academic and library buildings."
]

instance AirQualityForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive air quality forecasts including CO2 levels, VOC concentrations, and particulate matter trends with confidence intervals and recommended corrective actions."
]

instance StructuralHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural integrity analysis showing vibration patterns, pressure differentials, and equipment health status with anomaly detection and maintenance priority recommendations."
]

instance SystemStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of building systems showing real-time operational states, sensor readings, and control status with customizable dashboards for different user roles."
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Augmented and virtual reality visualizations enabling spatial inspection of building systems, equipment status overlays, and collaborative troubleshooting in immersive 3D environments."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendations for energy consumption optimization based on occupancy patterns, environmental data, and predictive analytics to improve efficiency in building operations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HVACAdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustments to HVAC system parameters (temperature setpoints, airflow rates) in response to real-time occupancy and environmental conditions to maintain optimal comfort and efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LightingAdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control of lighting systems based on occupancy detection and ambient light levels to optimize energy usage while maintaining adequate illumination standards."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance recommendations based on structural health monitoring data, including equipment inspection schedules and potential failure predictions with confidence levels."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ResourceOptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendations for resource optimization based on occupancy patterns and environmental data to improve efficiency in library operations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LightingSystemOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing optimization of lighting systems based on occupancy and ambient light conditions to maximize energy efficiency while maintaining adequate illumination."
    DTDFVocab:provides LightingEnergyInsight
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** in this DT system enables **real-time control commands** to be transmitted from the digital twin to physical building systems, executed by **facility managers** or automated processes. Control flows include **command types** such as HVAC adjustments, lighting regulation, or equipment activation, formatted in **standardized message structures** compatible with building automation systems. Validation and authentication mechanisms ensure **secure command execution**, with **feedback systems** providing confirmation of command receipt and execution status. Conflict resolution is handled via **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments to prioritize critical operations or mitigate inconsistencies. The framework supports **bidirectional feedback loops**, where physical system responses (e.g., sensor confirmations, error signals) are relayed back to the DT for continuous synchronization. Command execution confirmation is achieved through **event-driven acknowledgments**, ensuring operational integrity and traceability."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, implying sub-second to millisecond-level latency for critical operational data. The framework supports dynamic operational needs through **runtime configuration management**, enabling adjustments without full redeployment, which suggests adaptable synchronization frequencies tailored to service-specific requirements (e.g., sensor data updates, control commands, or analytics processing). While exact temporal granularity (e.g., per-second, per-minute) is not specified, the emphasis on **real-time** implies a high-resolution, low-latency synchronization model for operational monitoring and control, aligning with the AECO domain’s demand for immediate feedback loops between physical and digital systems. The absence of explicit latency thresholds or hardcoded frequencies indicates a design prioritizing flexibility over rigid time scales, with scalability considerations (NF_X) ensuring performance across heterogeneous components."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, role-based architecture** (as depicted in Figure 1) where the **DT Framework** serves as the central hub orchestrating interactions between three key roles: **Building Control Engineers** (responsible for BIM-based device modeling and initial deployment), **Facility Managers** (handling real-time monitoring/control), and **DT Engineers** (extending capabilities via custom service integration). This constellation leverages **bidirectional data exchange** to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic operational needs, ensuring a **holistic lifecycle management** approach. The framework’s **modular design** and **standardized extension points** facilitate **loose coupling** between components, enabling dynamic resource management (e.g., on-demand service activation) and system-wide coordination through implicit workflows (e.g., enriched BIM models → deployed DTs → operational monitoring). While no explicit orchestration protocols (e.g., microservices mesh, service orchestration engines) are mentioned, the emphasis on **runtime adaptability** and **low-overhead updates** implies a lightweight, event-driven coordination model tailored to the AECO domain’s evolving requirements."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans **five core phases**, each addressing distinct functional and operational requirements:
   1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define and configure **Building Control System (BCS) components** (sensors, actuators, controllers) within **BIM (Building Information Modeling) environments**, leveraging BIM’s static representations to create a **semantic foundation** for dynamic DT artifacts. This phase bridges the gap between BIM’s limitations and DT’s dynamic needs by enriching BIM models with **structured metadata** for subsequent transformations.
   2. **Model Transformation**: The framework employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract structured representations from enriched BIM models, automating the generation of **deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., runtime configurations, service interfaces) with **minimal manual intervention**. This phase ensures **seamless transition** from static BIM to dynamic DT representations via **semantic-preserving transformations**.
   3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed to operational environments, enabling **real-time synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital twins. This phase includes **automated provisioning** of DT services and **initial validation** of data exchange pipelines.
   4. **Runtime Operation**: The DT operates in **real-time**, with **continuous synchronization** facilitated by **real-time data exchange capabilities**. Facility Managers interact with the twin via **immersive visualization interfaces** (e.g., 3D BIM-integrated dashboards) for **monitoring and control**, while the DT’s **modular architecture** supports **runtime extensibility** through custom services.
   5. **Evolution and Maintenance**: The framework supports **lifecycle governance** through **modular extension points** and **standardized interfaces**, allowing DT Engineers to integrate **custom services, analytics, or plugins** to adapt to changing operational requirements. Version control and **incremental updates** are managed via the framework’s **modular architecture**, ensuring backward compatibility and scalability.

   **Representation Types**:
   - **Static (BIM)**: Used in early phases for device modeling and semantic enrichment.
   - **Dynamic (DT Artifacts)**: Generated via MDE for real-time operation and evolution.
   - **Hybrid (Runtime)**: Combines BIM visualizations with real-time data streams for immersive monitoring.

   **Transition Management**:
   - **Automated MDE pipelines** handle transitions between phases (e.g., BIM → DT artifacts).
   - **Standardized extension points** enable seamless integration of new services without disrupting existing workflows.
   - **Governance** is enforced via **role-based access** (e.g., Building Control Engineers for modeling, Facility Managers for operations)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **model fidelity** by bridging the gap between **static BIM representations** and **dynamic DT requirements**, ensuring that enriched BIM models accurately reflect physical building components and control logic. **Validation and verification** are implicit in the **MDE-driven deployment pipeline**, where structured representations are automatically generated and deployed with minimal manual intervention, reducing human error. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between BIM models and real-world conditions, but the framework mitigates this through **semantic consistency checks** during model transformation. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, though the **modular architecture** allows for **custom service integration** to incorporate advanced analytics or anomaly detection. **Quality assurance** is ensured by the **standardized extension points** and **runtime synchronization** between physical and digital systems, though specific metrics (e.g., accuracy thresholds, validation protocols) are not provided in the document."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **dt_technical_connection** infrastructure relies on a **modular, interoperable architecture** designed to support **heterogeneous system integration**. Communication protocols are **standardized** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or proprietary APIs) to enable seamless data exchange between BIM tools, IoT sensors, and building automation systems. The network topology follows a **centralized hub-and-spoke model**, where the DT Framework acts as the **central hub** facilitating bidirectional data flows. Bandwidth and latency requirements are optimized for **real-time synchronization**, with **scalability mechanisms** ensuring performance across individual components to entire infrastructure systems. Reliability and redundancy are achieved through **low-overhead updates** and **runtime configuration management**, minimizing downtime. Security protocols include **authentication, authorization, and encryption** to safeguard data integrity and confidentiality during transmission."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **dt_hosting_deployment** infrastructure supports **multi-platform deployment models**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise** configurations, tailored to operational needs. Computational and storage requirements are dynamically allocated based on **scalability demands**, with the framework ensuring **low-overhead updates** for maintainability. The deployment model leverages **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points**, enabling seamless integration of custom services and analytics. Availability and reliability are assured through **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments without full redeployment. The system prioritizes **accessibility** for users with varying technical expertise, while **interoperability** across heterogeneous platforms ensures compatibility with existing AECO workflows. Scalability is achieved via **distributed processing** and **modular service integration**, supporting evolution from individual components to entire infrastructure systems."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** (NF_X) to enable seamless **horizontal integration** with external tools and enterprise systems. Key integration patterns include:
  1. **Data Exchange Protocols**: Bidirectional synchronization between physical buildings (e.g., IoT sensors, building automation systems) and digital twins, though specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST APIs) are not named.
  2. **Interoperability Standards**: Alignment with **BIM tools** (used by Building Control Engineers for device modeling) and **operational systems** (e.g., facility management platforms), ensuring compatibility with static (BIM) and dynamic (real-time monitoring) data formats.
  3. **Dependencies**: The framework relies on **enriched BIM models** as the foundational input for DT deployment, implying a tight coupling with BIM software ecosystems (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) but no explicit dependencies on other enterprise systems (e.g., ERP, CMMS).
  4. **Integration with Other Digital Twins**: The modular architecture supports **custom service integration**, allowing the DT Framework to act as a gateway for cross-twin coordination (e.g., federated twins for multi-building campuses) or enterprise-wide analytics, though no specific standards (e.g., Digital Twin Consortium’s reference architecture) are referenced.
  The system’s **scalability** (NF_X) ensures integration remains viable across individual components to entire infrastructure systems, with **accessibility** considerations (NF_X) addressing user expertise gaps (e.g., abstracting complex protocols for Facility Managers)."
]
